title: 10 best herbal tinctures for acne

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander tinctures helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The tannins present in germander help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often underlying causes of acne. Additionally, the tincture's ability to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common skin inhabitant that contributes to acne, makes it an effective natural remedy for acne-prone skin. By addressing these root causes, germander tinctures can help to reduce acne severity and promote clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antibacterial properties: The tincture's antimicrobial compounds, like geraniol and linalool, can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to acne development and severity.

Antioxidant properties: Germander tincture's antioxidant flavonoids and phenolic acids may help neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress, which can lead to skin damage and inflammation, ultimately helping to prevent or alleviate acne.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinicine: This diterpenoid alkaloid has antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the growth of acne-causing bacteria on the skin.
Rosmarinic acid: A polyphenolic compound, rosmarinic acid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing redness and inflammation.
Carnosic acid: This diterpenoid has potent antioxidant properties, which can help protect the skin from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the severity of acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce acne and soothe the skin.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities, making them useful in acne treatment.
Barks: Rich in tannins and phenolic acids, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce acne inflammation and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh teucrium chamaedrys leaves in early morning when dew is still present on the leaves for maximum potency.
Clean the harvested leaves by gently brushing off dirt and debris from the leaves with a soft brush.
Dry the cleaned leaves by spreading them in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth for 1-2 weeks.
Steep the dried leaves in a solvent such as 80% ethanol at a 1:5 ratio for 2-3 weeks in a glass jar.
Filter the tincture through a coffee filter or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as lutein and zeaxanthin, work to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also targeting the underlying bacterial infection that can cause breakouts. By soothing and calming the skin, pot marigold tinctures help to unclog pores and promote a clearer complexion, making it an effective natural remedy for managing acne-prone skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, helping to soothe and calm the skin.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and flavonoids in pot marigold tincture have antimicrobial effects, which can help to combat bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a common cause of acne.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold tincture's antioxidant compounds can help to neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage and inflammation, reducing the appearance of acne scars and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce acne inflammation and prevent bacterial growth that can contribute to acne.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing redness and inflammation.

N-alkanoyl-4-hydroxyphenylalanine amide (also known as Napthoquinones): These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne and alleviate inflammation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They are used due to their astringent properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with acne.
Stems: They are used due to their antimicrobial properties, which help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause acne.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of fresh calendula flowers and 1 part of vegetable glycerin in a clean glass jar.
Combine the calendula flowers with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in the glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Store the herbal tincture in the refrigerator and use within 2 years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties. When applied topically or taken orally, sage's active compounds, such as thujone and camphor, can help to reduce the severity of acne by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that cause breakouts. Additionally, sage's anti-inflammatory properties may also soothe redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion and healthier skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne, thereby preventing breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and carnosol, can help to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of blemishes.

Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidants work to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, helping to protect the skin from oxidative stress and promote overall skin health.
remedy_constituents: Ursolic Acid: This triterpenoid helps with acne by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, reducing redness and preventing the growth of acne-causing bacteria.

Salvianolic Acid: A type of phenolic acid, salvianolic acid has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing the severity of acne and its symptoms.

Caryophyllene: A sesquiterpene with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, caryophyllene may help reduce inflammation and prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, promoting healthy skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Astringent and antimicrobial properties in the leaves help reduce acne inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.
Flowers: The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds in the flowers aid in reducing acne severity and promoting wound healing.
Stems: The stems contain antimicrobial and antioxidant compounds that help control acne-causing bacteria and soothe skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40g of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at dawn when the dew is still present.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area and enhance extraction efficiency.
Combine the chopped plant material with 50-60% ethanol or vodka in a 1:5 ratio by volume.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass container for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe tinctures helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The natural soothing agent also calms the skin, minimizing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, aloe's antimicrobial qualities help to combat bacteria that can cause acne, preventing clogged pores and reducing the appearance of blemishes. By addressing the root causes of acne, herbal aloe tinctures promote clear, healthy-looking skin and alleviate symptoms of this common skin concern.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne.

Antimicrobial properties: The tannins and anthraquinones in aloe vera exhibit antimicrobial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), the bacteria that contributes to acne development, thereby reducing the growth of these bacteria.

Wound healing properties: Aloe vera's high water content and enzymes promote tissue repair and collagen synthesis, which can help accelerate the healing process and reduce scarring associated with acne.
remedy_constituents: Anthraquinones: These compounds, particularly aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial growth that can cause acne.
Polysaccharides: The gel-like polysaccharides present in Aloe vera, such as acemannan, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Glycoproteins: The glycoproteins found in Aloe vera, like aloectin B, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help reduce acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their gel is rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves (outer skin): The outer skin of the leaves contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acne.
Leaves (inner gel): The inner gel of the leaves is high in vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which help to reduce inflammation and promote skin healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves with intact skin and trim the edges to remove any rough spots.
Dry the aloe vera leaves in a warm dry place for 7-10 days or until they become flexible.
Grind the dried aloe vera leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or blender.
Mix 1 part of the aloe vera powder with 2 parts of high-proof vodka or 80-proof rum in a clean glass jar.
Store the aloe vera tincture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with acne because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce redness and swelling, while its antibacterial agents combat the underlying causes of acne. The tannins present in dandelion also help to dry out excess oil and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of blackheads and whiteheads. Additionally, dandelion's antifungal capabilities prevent fungal overgrowth, a common contributor to acne. By addressing multiple aspects of acne, dandelion tinctures offer a natural and holistic solution for clearing up blemishes and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_properties: Diuretic properties: Dandelion's diuretic effect helps to reduce inflammation and excess oil production in the skin, which can contribute to acne by reducing congestion and promoting a healthy balance of moisture.
Antioxidant properties: Dandelion's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to skin cells, leading to acne and other skin issues.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, helping to soothe and calm the skin.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to reduce acne-causing inflammation and bacterial growth on the skin.

Luteolin: This flavonoid phenolic compound exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities, which can help to reduce oxidative stress, inflammation, and bacterial overgrowth that contribute to acne.

Taraxeryl acetate: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce acne-causing inflammation and bacterial growth on the skin, promoting a healthy balance of the skin's microbiome.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help to soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
Flowers: Flowers of the plant are used for their anti-oxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and fight off acne-causing bacteria.
Roots: Roots of Taraxacum officinale are used for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce inflammation in the skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for a fresh yield.
Chop 100g of the freshly harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a glass jar to create the extraction solution.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place to allow the active compounds to infuse into the ethanol.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a separate glass container to remove the plant material.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The apigenin present in chamomile has been shown to reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne, while also inhibiting the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that contribute to breakouts. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects calm irritated skin and reduce stress, which is a common acne trigger. By targeting multiple factors contributing to acne, chamomile tinctures offer a natural and effective way to promote clear and healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antimicrobial properties: Chamomile's antimicrobial properties, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as P. acnes, can help prevent bacterial overgrowth that contributes to acne development.
Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenoids have a calming effect on the skin, reducing irritation and stress-related inflammation that can exacerbate acne.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce redness and inflammation associated with acne.

Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help control acne-causing bacteria, reduce inflammation, and protect the skin from oxidative stress.

Matricine: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and prevent oxidative damage to the skin, both of which can contribute to the development of acne.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They contain apigenin and other flavonoids that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne.
Roots: They contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent acne.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and 2 parts of a solvent such as 80 proof vodka.
Combine the dried flowers and solvent in a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid and label it clearly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild geranium tinctures helps with acne because of its unique ability to balance hormones, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy skin. The plant's bioactive compounds work to regulate sebum production, unclog pores, and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, wild geranium has antibacterial properties that help combat the bacteria that can cause acne. By using a herbal tincture, individuals can naturally address the root causes of acne and achieve clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Wild geranium's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, making it an effective treatment for inflammatory acne.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of wild geranium tincture help combat bacterial overgrowth on the skin, which can cause acne, reducing the severity of breakouts and promoting healing.

Antioxidant: Wild geranium's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to acne, and also promote collagen production, improving skin elasticity and reducing the appearance of scars.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help with acne by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne.

Phenolic acids (including gallic acid and ellagic acid): These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help control acne-causing bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) and reduce the risk of infection.
Tannins: Tannins in Geranium maculatum have astringent properties, which can help reduce sebum production and tighten pores, making them less prone to clogging and acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Geranium maculatum contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce acne inflammation and prevent bacterial growth.
Leaves: The leaves of Geranium maculatum contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Flowers: The flowers of Geranium maculatum contain fragrances and flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, helping to reduce acne lesions and prevent infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh geranium maculatum roots and leaves on a sunny day in late spring.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 week.
Use a clean glass jar to combine the dried plant material with 2 cups of vodka as a solvent.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking of the jar.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in lemon balm, such as rosmarinic acid and citral, work to reduce inflammation and combat bacteria that cause acne, promoting a clearer and healthier complexion. Additionally, lemon balm's ability to soothe and calm the skin can help alleviate redness and irritation associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for achieving radiant and balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: Lemon balm's antibacterial properties can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, which are a primary cause of acne, reducing inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Lemon balm's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also soothing irritated skin and promoting healing.

Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to acne development, such as environmental stressors and UV damage, which can lead to premature aging and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that acts as an antioxidant, reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the skin, which can contribute to acne development.
Limonene: A terpene with antimicrobial properties that helps to reduce the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that can cause acne.

Volatile oils (mainly linalool and linalyl acetate): Terpenes with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the skin, which can lead to acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain antioxidants and flavonoids, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with acne.
Flowers: They have antimicrobial properties, which can help control bacterial and fungal infections that can lead to acne.
Leaves: They also contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which can help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of melissa leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for better quality.
Chop 1 part of the fresh plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking for optimal extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb has been traditionally used to treat skin issues, including pimples and acne, by reducing redness and swelling associated with these conditions. The tincture also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects that help soothe the skin and reduce inflammation. Additionally, St John's Wort has antibacterial properties that can help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria, a common cause of acne. By addressing these underlying causes, St John's Wort tinctures can provide natural relief from acne symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial activity: St. John's Wort's antibacterial properties can help combat Pseudomonas aeruginosa, a common bacteria that causes acne by clogging pores and causing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids and hyperforin in St. John's Wort have anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with acne, promoting healing and reducing scarring.

Antioxidant activity: St. John's Wort's antioxidant properties can help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development, oxidative stress, and premature aging.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-derived compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with acne, promoting a healthier skin environment.
Naphthodianthrones (e.g., Hyperforin): This class of compounds has shown antimicrobial properties that can help combat acne-causing bacteria, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes).
Phenolic acids (e.g., Quercetin): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and calm acne-prone skin, reducing redness and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, particularly quercetin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce acne inflammation.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin, a naphthodianthrones compound with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help combat acne-causing bacteria and reduce redness.
Stems: Contain hypericin, a naphthodianthrones compound with antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from damage and promote healing, reducing acne severity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 percent dry weight of st john's wort flowers and leaves on a sunny day in summer.
Clean the herb thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris from the surface.
Combine 100 grams of the cleaned herb with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon tinctures helps with acne because of its natural antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The citric acid in lemon has been shown to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and kill bacteria that cause breakouts. The tannins present in the herbal extract also help to reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne. Additionally, the astringent properties of lemon can help to dry out excess oil and reduce the appearance of acne scars. As a result, incorporating herbal lemon tinctures into one's skincare routine can lead to clearer, healthier-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Lemon balm, a common ingredient in herbal lemon tinctures, has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes, which is a primary cause of acne, reducing inflammation and preventing infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemons contain flavonoids like naringenin, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting healthy skin and tissue repair.

Antioxidant properties: The high concentration of vitamin C and other antioxidants in lemon tinctures helps to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to acne development, protecting the skin from oxidative stress and environmental damage.
remedy_constituents: Naringenin: This flavanone has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth associated with acne.
Limonene: A terpene with antimicrobial properties, limonene can help combat the bacterial overgrowth that contributes to acne, particularly P. acnes.
Hesperidin: A flavanone with strong antioxidant properties, hesperidin may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to the improvement of acne-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are often used because of their flavonoid content, which has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in reducing acne inflammation and preventing further breakouts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of organic citrus limon peels in late summer or early fall when they are most fragrant.
Dry the peels in a single layer on paper bags or a drying rack for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1:5 to 1:10 ratio of dried peels to 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids to create the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The menthol present in peppermint also has antiseptic and antibacterial qualities that help combat P. acnes bacteria, a common cause of acne. Additionally, peppermint's cooling effect can ease the discomfort and itching often experienced during the healing process. By addressing these underlying issues, herbal peppermint tinctures can provide natural relief from acne symptoms and promote clearer, healthier skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Peppermint tincture's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), thereby preventing clogged pores and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, like menthol and menthone, help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, promoting a smoother skin tone and reduced acne severity.

Antioxidant properties: Peppermint tincture's antioxidant properties neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and damage to the skin, which can contribute to the development of acne, and also help to protect the skin from environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, peppermint roots help reduce inflammation and prevent acne breakouts.
Stems: Stems contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antibacterial properties, making them effective against acne-causing bacteria.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or purchase organic dried peppermint leaves in bulk.
Chop the dried peppermint leaves into small pieces and combine them with vodka or rum in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and retaining the tincture liquid.
Store the peppermint tincture in an amber glass bottle away from direct sunlight at room temperature for up to 2 years for use as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with acne because its natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness and swelling, while its antimicrobial agents combat bacterial overgrowth that can clog pores. The tincture's ability to stimulate digestion and eliminate toxins also helps to unclog the skin from within, reducing the likelihood of breakouts. Additionally, wormwood's antiseptic qualities help to soothe and calm irritated skin, promoting a clear and radiant complexion.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial compounds, such as thujone and camphor, help to combat bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is a common cause of acne by reducing inflammation and preventing infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in wormwood tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with acne, making it an effective natural remedy for soothing and calming the skin.

Antioxidant properties: Wormwood's antioxidant compounds help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to inflammation, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of acne scars and blemishes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, which help to dry up pimples and reduce inflammation.
Seeds: Containing volatile oils with antimicrobial properties, which aid in fighting bacteria that cause acne.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried artemisia absinthium leaves and vodka for the tincture preparation.
Combine one part of dried herb with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four weeks.
After four weeks strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container.
Transfer the tincture to a dropper bottle and label it with the ingredients and date.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The tincture's ability to reduce inflammation can help soothe red and irritated skin, while its antioxidant properties can help combat free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and contribute to acne development. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to have a natural antibacterial effect, which can help reduce the growth of bacteria that can clog pores and cause breakouts. By using herbal stinging nettle tincture, individuals may experience a reduction in acne severity and frequency.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tincture has anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans, which can reduce inflammation in the skin, helping to calm redness and swelling associated with acne.

Antibacterial properties: The tannins and saponins present in stinging nettle tincture have antibacterial effects, making it effective against bacterial strains like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), which is often linked to acne development.

Antioxidant properties: Stinging nettle's antioxidant compounds help protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and preventing skin cell damage that can contribute to acne formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing redness and inflammation associated with acne.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and saponins that help reduce sebum production and prevent clogged pores, making them a great addition to acne treatment tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves at dawn or just before dusk to ensure optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested nettle leaves thoroughly with cold running water to remove impurities.
Combine one part dried nettle leaves with three parts vodka in a clean glass jar, filling to the brim.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to extract the medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The saponins present in marshmallows have been shown to reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also helping to calm and reduce inflammation. Additionally, the tincture's natural astringent properties can help to shrink pores and tighten skin, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone areas of the face.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Marshmallow tincture's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, allowing for a faster healing process.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tincture can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to skin damage and inflammation caused by acne, promoting healthy skin cells.

Soothing and Protective Mucilage: Marshmallow's mucilage coats and protects the skin, providing a soothing barrier that helps calm irritation and prevents further bacterial growth, making it an effective treatment for acne-prone skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, the roots are used to create a soothing and calming tincture that helps reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne.
Seeds: Containing high levels of flavonoids, the seeds provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help combat acne-causing bacteria and soothe irritated skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried althaea officinalis leaves and flowers according to the botanical specifications.
Wash the plant material with filtered water to remove dirt and impurities gently for 10 minutes.
Chop the cleaned plant material into smaller pieces to ensure proper saturation of menstruum slowly for 30 minutes.
Combine one part chopped althaea officinalis with three parts high-proof ethanol in a clean glass jar tightly sealable immediately.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 6 weeks shaking the jar daily for optimal extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato tinctures helps with acne because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. The tincture's antimicrobial properties also help to combat P. acnes bacteria, a common culprit behind acne, thereby preventing future occurrences of inflammation. Additionally, the tincture's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can cause skin damage, promoting healthy skin cell growth and regeneration, resulting in clearer and more balanced skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wild potato tincture contains compounds that inhibit the growth of bacteria, viruses, and fungi, which can help reduce the severity of acne by preventing infections and reducing inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds can help soothe and calm red, swollen, and irritated skin, reducing acne's painful and unsightly symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Wild potato tincture is rich in antioxidants that neutralize free radicals, which can damage skin cells and lead to acne. By scavenging these harmful molecules, the tincture helps protect the skin from oxidative stress and promotes overall skin health.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the wild potato plant are used due to their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing acne inflammation and fighting off bacterial infections.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, of the wild potato plant contain compounds that help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne, while also exhibiting antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried taraxacum kok saghyz roots and cut them into small pieces to prepare for extraction.
Combine the root pieces with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar and seal it tightly for a maceration period of two weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid tincture.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool, dry place for at least four months to allow for aging and maturation.
Once matured, use the tincture as directed by diluting it with water or other carrier liquids according to a ratio of 1:3 to 1:5 for acne treatment applications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Jesuit's bark tinctures helps with acne because it contains berberine, a compound that has natural antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Berberine works to reduce the severity of acne by killing bacteria that cause breakouts and reducing inflammation in the skin. Additionally, Jesuit's bark tincture may also help to balance hormones, which can contribute to hormonal acne. By incorporating this herbal remedy into your skincare routine, you can experience a reduction in acne symptoms and enjoy clear, healthy-looking skin.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Jesuit's Bark Tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a type of bacteria that contributes to acne, thereby helping to reduce inflammation and prevent further bacterial growth.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture contains flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with acne, and also alleviate the discomfort and pain caused by acne lesions.

Antioxidant properties: Jesuit's Bark Tincture contains antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of acne by damaging skin cells and causing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, roots help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation associated with acne.
Barks: Containing salicin, a natural derivative of aspirin, barks have been traditionally used to reduce redness and swelling caused by acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality cinchona officinalis leaves at dawn when dew is still present for maximum potency.
Rinse the harvested leaves thoroughly with distilled water and air-dry them in a warm, dark place for 24 hours.
Combine dried cinchona officinalis leaves with vodka in a ratio of 1:5, ensuring a minimum of 20% proof concentration.
Steep the mixture at room temperature for 14 days, shaking the container daily to facilitate extraction and infusion.
Strain and filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles, storing it in a cool, dark place for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Willow herb tinctures helps with acne because of its unique ability to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth. The herbal extract's salicylic acid content helps to unclog pores, while its antimicrobial properties prevent future breakouts. Additionally, willow herb's anti-inflammatory effects soothe redness and itching associated with acne-prone skin, reducing the appearance of pimples and promoting a clearer complexion. By addressing both internal and external factors, willow herb tinctures offer a holistic approach to tackling acne from within.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal willow herb tinctures contain salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne.
Antioxidant: Willow herb's antioxidant properties help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to acne development.
Antimicrobial: The tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) bacteria that thrive on oily skin, reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of willow herb are rich in salicylic acid, which helps to reduce inflammation and unclog pores, making them an effective ingredient in acne treatments.
Flowers: Willow herb flowers contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, helping to combat acne-causing bacteria and soothe skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh centaurium erythraea roots and clean them thoroughly removing any dirt or debris.
Chop the cleaned roots into small pieces to increase surface area for solvent interaction.
Combine one part chopped root material with 2 parts high-proof vodka in a sterilized glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to steep for 6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into another sterilized glass bottle discarding the solids and label.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with acne because it possesses antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties that combat the root causes of breakouts. The menthol content in wintergreen helps to reduce sebum production, unclog pores, and soothe irritated skin. Additionally, its antioxidant compounds work to neutralize free radicals and promote healthy collagen production, resulting in a reduction in acne severity and inflammation. By incorporating herbal wintergreen tinctures into one's skincare routine, individuals can experience a significant improvement in their complexion.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This key compound in wintergreen has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and bacterial growth on the skin, thus combating acne.

Wintergreen's natural antioxidants: Wintergreen contains a high concentration of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals that can cause oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which are associated with acne.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Wintergreen tincture also contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps reduce inflammation in the skin, minimizing redness and swelling caused by acne.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in salicylic acid, the roots help to reduce inflammation and ease redness associated with acne.
Barks: The barks' high concentration of methyl salicylate provides natural antibacterial properties, making it effective against P. acnes bacteria that causes acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in the morning for optimal potency.
Weigh out 100 grams of plant material and chop it into small pieces for efficient extraction.
Combine chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 2 weeks at room temperature, shaking daily, then strain the liquid.
Filter the resulting tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle, store in a cool place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Allspice tinctures helps with acne because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as allyl isothiocyanate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), a bacteria that causes acne. remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Allspice tincture's antibacterial compounds, such as eugenol and caryophyllene, help combat acne-causing bacteria like Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes), reducing inflammation and preventing future breakouts.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in allspice tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with acne, promoting a clearer complexion.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pimenta dioica leaves and stems in the morning when essential oils are most concentrated.
Rinse the plant material thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Chop the rinsed plant material into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction carefully.
Combine chopped plant material with 70% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1:5 for 2 weeks steeping time.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and store the resulting tincture in dark glass bottles.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for acne are concentrated plant extracts that help alleviate acne symptoms by reducing inflammation, fighting bacteria, and promoting skin health. The best herbal tinctures for acne include Calendula officinalis, Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Silybum marianum, Echinacea purpurea, Hypericum perforatum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe vera, Zingiber officinale, and Taraxacum officinale. If left untreated, acne can lead to emotional distress, low self-esteem, and social withdrawal, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to acne that are treated with herbal tinctures are eczema, rosacea, and psoriasis. Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, such as tea tree oil, echinacea, and calendula, can help soothe and calm irritated skin. They may also be used to treat other skin issues like boils, abscesses, and folliculitis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with acne is a blend of tea tree oil, calendula, and neem. Tea tree oil has antiseptic and antibacterial properties, reducing inflammation and fighting acne-causing bacteria. Calendula soothes and calms the skin, reducing redness and promoting wound healing. Neem tincture, rich in antioxidants, helps to balance the skin's natural pH and prevent future breakouts. When taken together, these tinctures can effectively heal and prevent acne, promoting clear and healthy skin. Consult a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Evidence-based complementary and alternative medicine: eCAM, herbal tinctures are good for acne because they have been shown to significantly reduce both inflammatory and non-inflammatory acne lesions. The study found that out of 34 clinical trials involving 1753 participants, most trials demonstrated the efficacy of herbal medicine in treating acne vulgaris. Additionally, some medicinal plants were found to have equal or even higher efficacy compared to standard treatments, suggesting their potential as a promising treatment strategy for mild to moderate acne.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis tinctures helps with acne because of its powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's ability to soothe and calm irritated skin reduces redness and inflammation associated with acne. The plant's antiseptic qualities also help combat bacterial overgrowth, a common cause of acne. Additionally, calendula's antioxidant properties help protect skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a balanced skin ecosystem and reducing the severity of acne breakouts. This makes calendula a popular natural remedy for acne-prone skin.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Help with acne by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby preventing the formation of acne lesions and promoting healing.
Carotenoids: Help with acne by acting as antioxidants, reducing the formation of free radicals that can cause inflammation and oxidative damage to skin cells.
Saponins: Help with acne by having antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the growth of bacteria such as Propionibacterium acnes (P. acnes) that contribute to acne development.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are commonly used because they contain flavonoids and triterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe and heal acne-prone skin.
Leaves: They are used because they contain similar compounds to the flowers, which help to reduce inflammation and fight bacterial and fungal infections that can cause acne.
Stems: They are also used because they contain a combination of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to regulate the skin's natural balance and reduce inflammation and redness associated with acne.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers in full bloom when they are completely open and dry the flowers immediately using paper bags.
Weigh out 1 part dried calendula flowers to 2 parts vodka or 75 proof grain alcohol and combine.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar with a lid and let it steep for 2 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Store the calendula tincture in a cool dark place and let it mature for 2 weeks before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with acne because of its potent antimicrobial properties, which effectively combat bacterial overgrowths that contribute to acne formation. The tannins and flavonoids present in the tincture also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing redness and swelling associated with acne. Additionally, its astringent properties help to balance skin pH, prevent clogged pores, and promote healthy skin cell regeneration, ultimately leading to a reduction in acne severity and frequency, promoting clearer and healthier skin.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This monoterpene helps reduce acne by its antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria that cause acne.
Caryophyllene: This sesquiterpene oxide has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.
Linalool: This terpene has antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help prevent infections and reduce the severity of acne.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antibacterial and antifungal compounds, which help in reducing acne-causing bacteria and inflammation.
Buds: They contain antimicrobial properties that help in preventing the growth of acne-causing bacteria and promoting healing of the skin.
Flowers: They are a rich source of melaleuca oil, which has antibacterial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing acne and promoting healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia at the peak of their potency and aroma.
Dry the leaves in a warm dark place for 1-2 weeks to reduce their moisture content.
Combine the dried leaves with 500ml of 40% ethanol in a clean glass jar and seal it.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking it daily to ensure extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 percent organic aloe barbadensis leaves by cutting them at base for maximum potency.
Chop the harvested aloe barbadensis leaves into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 1 part aloe barbadensis leaf pieces with 2 parts 80 proof ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for maximum extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container to separate liquid from solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum tinctures helps with acne because they contain silymarin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation and soothes the skin. remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of dried silybum marianum flowers and 2 parts of dried silybum marianum seeds.
Combine the harvested plant material with 80% high-proof ethanol in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the container every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Store the herbal tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or longer when sealed properly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce redness and swelling associated with breakouts. Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material into small pieces and set aside for 30 minutes to allow for drying.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar with a tight lid.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow for maceration and tincture formation.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum tinctures helps with acne because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe redness and swelling associated with acne. remedy_recipe: Harvest 25-30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and cut them into small pieces for tincture preparation.
Combine the glycyrrhiza glabra pieces with 500 milliliters of 80 percent vodka in a clean glass container.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks to allow for solvent extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container to remove solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. It reduces redness and swelling associated with acne, while also fighting the bacteria that cause breakouts. remedy_parts: Leaves: The most commonly used part of Aloe vera in tinctures for acne, as they contain high amounts of gel and juices rich in anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.

Barks: Some Aloe vera species have medicinal barks that contain compounds like aloin, which has been traditionally used to treat skin conditions, including acne, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from mature plants at least 2 years old for maximum potency.
Wash the leaves thoroughly with filtered water to remove dirt and debris.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and soak them in 80 proof vodka in a 1 part aloe to 2 parts vodka ratio for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years and use 20-30 drops as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with acne because of its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The active compound gingerol in ginger root has been shown to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth, common causes of acne. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce redness and swelling associated with acne.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes and clean them thoroughly to remove dirt and debris.
Dry the harvested rhizomes in a single layer at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours to remove moisture.
Chop the dried rhizomes into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase the surface area for extraction.
Steep the chopped rhizomes in 4 cups of 80% ethanol for 2 weeks in a sealed container to create the tincture.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids to produce a clear liquid herbal extract.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20 parts of taraxacum officinale roots and 1 part of vegetable glycerin for tincture preparation.
Chop the taraxacum officinale roots into small pieces using a sharp knife for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar for maceration.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with occasional shaking for extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and store it in a cool dark place.
